introSection: Bulldog Coaster is a steel roller coaster located at Brean Theme Park in Brean, Somerset, England, UK. It is one of the first looping roller coasters built by Pinfari.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster dates back to 1982. Before arriving in the United Kingdom, the roller coaster travelled in Switzerland with showman Paul Doesegger.

It opened at Flamingo Land in 1986. It closed after the 1995 season and was subsequently replaced by the Top Gun. Crazy Loop was later refurbished and relocated to Pleasure Island Family Theme Park, where it operated from 1996 to 2002 on the former location of Four Man Bob. It was subsequently removed after the 2002 season and was put up for sale in October 2002. It was then replaced by Pendulus in 2003.

It was then sold and relocated to Brean Theme Park, where it was once again refurbished. The ride was renamed Shockwave. It opened in 2004.

In 2014 or earlier, the name was returned to Crazy Loop.

The ride closed in 2021 and was relocated within the park. It reopened as Bulldog Coaster in 2023.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Has a single vertical loop and a helix.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Red track and blue supports. The ride's track used to be painted yellow, but was repainted in between 2013 and 2015. The coaster had red track and white supports before arriving at Brean Theme Park.

fullText:
Bulldog Coaster is a steel roller coaster located at Brean Theme Park in Brean, Somerset, England, UK. It is one of the first looping roller coasters built by Pinfari.

The roller coaster dates back to 1982. Before arriving in the United Kingdom, the roller coaster travelled in Switzerland with showman Paul Doesegger.

It opened at Flamingo Land in 1986. It closed after the 1995 season and was subsequently replaced by the Top Gun. Crazy Loop was later refurbished and relocated to Pleasure Island Family Theme Park, where it operated from 1996 to 2002 on the former location of Four Man Bob. It was subsequently removed after the 2002 season and was put up for sale in October 2002. It was then replaced by Pendulus in 2003.

It was then sold and relocated to Brean Theme Park, where it was once again refurbished. The ride was renamed Shockwave. It opened in 2004.

In 2014 or earlier, the name was returned to Crazy Loop.

The ride closed in 2021 and was relocated within the park. It reopened as Bulldog Coaster in 2023.

Has a single vertical loop and a helix.

Red track and blue supports. The ride's track used to be painted yellow, but was repainted in between 2013 and 2015. The coaster had red track and white supports before arriving at Brean Theme Park.
